member.name: F:Core.BalanceCalAdv.enumBalCalAxisToCalIndicesToDoCrossTerm
member.summary: This is a mapping from enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices to the doCrossTerm array The first element in each pair is the x coordinate, the second element is the y coordinate in the doCrossTerm array. the desired pair is selected by a enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices value.

member.name: M:Core.BalanceCalAdv.addHighOrderTerms(MatrixLibrary.Matrix,System.Int32,MatrixLibrary.Matrix@,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: calulates and adds the high order terms to H and G, which are expected to be H[(balanceCal as BalanceCalAdv).HighOrderTerms, 1] and G[numberOfAxis, numberOfAxis + (balanceCal as BalanceCalAdv).HighOrderTerms] H contains only the high order bridge output terms, for a point of interest. G contains the load information for all axis, both orginal loads and the high order terms related to the orginal loads
member.param: either G or dR, assumed to start in row 0
the matrix in which to place the higher order terms
the column in which to add the higher order terms
the row in the target in which to start adding the higher order terms

member.name: F:Core.FBalCal.hasMomentComponent5f1m
member.summary: a convience array that makes it easy to know if a selected enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices has a moment component. Used to determine if the old style moment loading method (which also loads N1 and N2), or the pure moment loading should be used to adjust the N1/N2 loads. Notice the one-for-one correspondance with enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices, this way enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices can be used to index into this array to determine if there is a moment component.

member.name: P:Core.HwControllerTraverse_HWside.ucPositionX
member.summary: Position X interface between the UControllerTraverse and the HwControllerTraverse. UNIT CONVERSION is done here, since the HWControllerTraverse works in native units, and Everything else works in User Specified Units. NOTE: keep this a 'protected' float, so that it can ONLY be used within this class and its sub-classes. If it were public and UControllerTraverse tried to use it the units conversion would work backwards.

member.name: P:Core.HwControllerTraverse_HWside.ucPositionY
member.summary: Position Y interface between the UControllerTraverse and the HwControllerTraverse. UNIT CONVERSION is done here, since the HWControllerTraverse works in native units, and Everything else works in User Specified Units. NOTE: keep this a 'protected' float, so that it can ONLY be used within this class and its sub-classes. If it were public and UControllerTraverse tried to use it the units conversion would work backwards.

member.name: P:Core.HwControllerTraverse_HWside.ucPositionZ
member.summary: Position Z interface between the UControllerTraverse and the HwControllerTraverse. UNIT CONVERSION is done here, since the HWControllerTraverse works in native units, and Everything else works in User Specified Units. NOTE: keep this a 'protected' float, so that it can ONLY be used within this class and its sub-classes. If it were public and UControllerTraverse tried to use it the units conversion would work backwards.

member.summary: used to switch between a 1 Torr and 10 Torr full scale pressure transducer i.e. if 10 Torr = 10 Volts, use a value of 1 if 10 Torr = 1 Volt, use a value of 10

member.summary: VelocityController is Running

member.name: M:Core.HwControllerVelocity_HWside.timerVelocityCmndActive_Tick(System.Object,System.EventArgs)
member.summary: the velocity controller is a bit different than the sting, or traverse, controllers since the fan keeps running until the end of the test. The sting and traverse controllers are only active, while the respective controller is moving to a new position.

member.name: F:Core.HwControllerVelocity_HWside.currentDemoVelocity
member.summary: LEAVE THIS PRIVATE SINCE IT SHOULD ONLY BE USED IN THIS CLASS for demo purposes

member.name: P:Core.HwControllerVelocity_HWside.ucVelocity
member.summary: Current Velocity interface between the UControllerVelocity and the HwControllerVelocity. UNIT CONVERSION is done here, since the HWControllerVelocity works in native units, and Everything else works in User Specified Units. NOTE: keep this a 'protected' float, so that it can ONLY be used within this class and its sub-classes. If it were public and UControllerVelocity tried to use it the units conversion would work backwards.

member.name: M:Core.HwControllerVelocity_HWside.SetFlowVelocity(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Calculate the flow velocity. THIS IS CALLED FROM THE DataCollector as it reads the current pressure and temperature.
member.param: the Vdiff Voltage pressure reading
the Temperature reading in degrees C.

member.summary: The maximum allowed value

member.summary: The minimum allowed value

member.summary: The currentValue in the TextBox

member.summary: Set the currentValue in the TextBox, optioanl bounds checking

member.returns: returns 'true' if 'value' is valid.

member.summary: Set the currentValue to the value string, if it is a valid string and within the MaxValue and MinValue bounds.

member.returns: returns true if a valid, within blunds string.

member.name: T:Core.GroupBoxFOa
member.summary: GroupBoxFOa - this is a specialized helper class for the Flow OFF and Flow ON GroupBox's It is TestState aware and Flow OFF/ON aware, hence the xCmRow GroupBoxFOa (GroupBox Flow OFF/ON aware)

member.name: T:Core.IToolUSAFAControllersMethods
member.summary: NOTE 1: The interfaces required for any Tool that wants to control the SSwt and/or the TSwt stings. The issue is wether the Tool knows about the specific Controller, or the Controller knows about the specific Tool. Such as: The IOTech 3000PDAQ or the VXI can be used to control the SSWT Sting, but the two Tools have a different internal architecture. The VXI has two separate devices (1413 and 1415), where the IOTech is treated as one device. Both having the Analog Voltage In, Analog Voltage Out, and Digital I/O that is required to control the SSWT Sting. This class is the result of the way that I partitioned this problem. NOTE 2: The other issue is the desire to keep specific driver knowledge (i.e. *.dll modules) out of 'Core'. The reason for this was so that only the necessary drivers need to be installed in a specific tunnel.

member.name: M:Core.IToolUSAFAControllersMethods.LSWT_SetAbbACB530_RPM(System.Int16)
member.summary: Sets the scaled RPM value (speed) in the ABB ACB 530 Drive Control The scale is 0 - 20000, which maps 0 - full speed (60Hz/~115fps)

member.summary: used to prevent infinite looping

member.name: M:Core.UcmboBxEnumList.addCmboBxLabelsNumeric``1(``0)
member.summary: Allows a list of voltages, frequencies, etc to be represented as as enum, and converted to a user friendly format. Consider the following enum enum AiVxiVoltageRanges { Auto, V00625, V025, V1, V4, V16 } expects a special type of enum where the identifiers start with a alpha character(s) followed by numeric character(s). Such as V4, in this case the alpha character, 'V', will be moved from the end of the character string, which will yield the string "4 V". If the numeric portion of an identifier starts with a '0', such as "V00625", then that will be interpreted as "0.0625 V". Exception: the identifier with no numeric characters will not be modified, such as 'Auto' will not be converted. NOTE: a identifier like 'A2B3' is going to get unexpected results with this method

member.summary: This method is specific to enum ToolTypes

member.summary: returns the enum value of the curently selected index in the items list

member.name: F:Core.FBalanceCalibrator.hasMomentComponent5f1m
member.summary: a convience array that makes it easy to know if a selected enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices has a moment component. Used to determine if the old style moment loading method (which also loads N1 and N2), or the pure moment loading should be used to adjust the N1/N2 loads. Notice the one-for-one correspondance with enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices, this way enumBalCalAxisToCalIndices can be used to index into this array to determine if there is a moment component.

member.name: T:Core.FileType
member.summary: the file types that TunnelVision knows about. TunnelDefinition - Contains the HW and setup unique to a specific Tunnel. TestPoint - a file containing a Test Point Matrix. See Test Point File in the online Help, tvTng.chm Configuration - Saves and Restore the User defined configuration parameters.

member.name: T:Core.TestState
member.summary:
used to indicate the current state of ButtonTestState. EditTestPoints - The initial mode that allows editing of the Test Point matrix TestPending - Getting ready to test, Test Point matrix changes not allowed TestRunning - Execution of the Test Point matrix
CurrentState -> EditTestPoints TestPending SetupForNextTestPoint DataCollectionInProgress button Pressed buttonTestState goto TestPending goto EditTestPoints goto TestPending goto TestPending buttonManualContinue disabled goto SetupForNextTestPoint goto DataCollectionInProgress disabled (used for Manual Continue)

member.name: T:Core.TestExpectedEventCompleteType
member.summary: Used to indicate who/why the event is being raised ContinueButton - the manualContinueButton has been clicked by the USER PauseDuration - the Pause Duration timerSendQueue has expired TestPointDataToFileComplete - the calData for the current Test Point has been saved to disk StingMoveComplete - the autoSting has positioned the sting to the requested position TraverseMoveComplete - the autoTraverse has positioned the traverse to the requested position

member.name: T:Core.ExpectedEventDelegate
member.summary: Used by RaiseExpectedEvent() to raise events that cause the StateMachine to progress to the next state. Events are raised by the various automated XController, the ManualContinueButton, the HW Data Collectors to signal they have collected the designated calData, and by the calData store routine to indicate that the calData has been saved.

member.name: T:Core.FTool
member.summary: a stand alone form that controls a xTool. A XTool being an abstract instantiation of an entity that can provide test functionality, such as data collection, controllers such as a Sting Controller or a Traverse Controller. DISCLAIMER: FTool and UTool contain some redundant functionality, and could have been designed with a common base class. However, since FTool is derived from class Form and UTool is based on class UserControl, it would difficult to make a base class for FTool and UTool.

member.name: M:Core.FTool.initializeFTool
member.summary: initialization for the FTool, non-internet related

member.name: M:Core.FTool.establishTcpUdpConnection(System.String[])
member.summary: internet related initialization for the FTool. NOTE: the same port number is used for both the TCP and the UDP connections. The TCP socket is used for normal communication between TvTng and the FTool. The UDP socket is used for "Stop" and "Exit"
member.param: the IP/TcpPort/UdpPort information

member.name: F:Core.FTool.remoteExecution
member.summary: true indicates that this instantiation of FTool is threadReceiveRunning as a remote slave

member.name: M:Core.FTool.getHwControllerVelocityLSWT
member.summary: This gets overridden in FTool_LSWT_Flow_Velocity, so the the correct HwControllerVelocityACB530 is instantiated.

member.name: M:Core.FTool.stopTest
member.summary: This is called at the end of each test. This is used for data collectors, like the VXI, which are prone to overrunning their data buffer. If the data collector is also used as a controller (LSWT Velocity), or monitor (SSWT Monitor), Then you shouldn't stop, since it will stop the controller / monitor from working.

member.name: P:Core.FTool.ReadyForTestRun

member.name: M:Core.FTool.mergeHwChannels(Core.XTool)

member.param: the new config to merge with
member.returns: This is a warning. true means clean merge, no conflicts found.

member.summary: setup valid channels for the LSWT Flow Velocity Controller. On the UTool side make sure that the channels have been specified in the UDevice class. member.name: T:Core.ReadBufferState
member.summary: The state of the Read Buffer

member.name: T:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1
member.summary: a circular buffer primarily used to collect calData from a calData collector such as the HP1413, and give each calData reader a chance to use the calData.
member.remarks: This generic was written with the knowledge that the HW returns an array of floats for each sample It is possible that there are multiple samples for each 'dataBlock' returned from the HW So sizeof(dataBlock) = numberOfLoadings * numberOfSampleElements; doing this should allow the calData from the HW to be read directly into the dataBlock. NOTE: this generic has only been tested with float, since that the type returned from the HW It should work with any primitive type, such as int, int64, double. More complex types will need additional testing

member.name: T:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.DataHeader
member.summary: The header for each block of calData returned from calData collector

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.DataHeader.next
member.summary: Index into the circular buffer of the next calData chunk

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.DataHeader.prev
member.summary: Index into the circular buffer of the previous calData chunk

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.DataHeader.readerThreadCount
member.summary: The number of threads that are interested in this calData chunk. If 0 then it is free for re-use

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.DataHeader.sizeOfDataBlock
member.summary: The size of the dataBlock = numberOfLoadings * numberOfSampleElements

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.DataHeader.dataBlock
member.summary: A dataBlock is an array of dataSamples, NOTE: I put them into one contiguous array since the HP returns them this way.

member.name: M:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.DataHeader.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Instantiate a DataHeader block of memory for use in the circular buffer.
member.param: The number of sample if this dataBlock.
The number of SamplesElements, curAxis.listStrings. Channels, in this dataBlock.

member.name: T:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.BufferState
member.summary: a lockable class, indicating the state of the circular buffer, that protects access to the contents.

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.BufferState.currentHead
member.summary: the index of the dataBlock that will be filled next by the calData collector.

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.BufferState.readerCount
member.summary: The number of readers that have registered with the circular buffer.

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.BufferState.dataWindowMaxSize
member.summary: The maximum size that the dataWindow will be allowed to grow. The dataWindow is the number of dataBlocks that will not be released.
member.remarks: This is to allow the oscope to find the last starting point after a screen resize. NOTE: all calData readers using this circular buffer will have the same window size.

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.BufferState.dataWindowCount
member.summary: Then current number of dataBlocks that are currently being held for a reader. After startup this will grow to equal dataWindowMaxSize.

member.summary: The number of samples per dataBlock

member.name: F:Core.InstrumentDataBuffer`1.numberOfSampleElements
member.summary: The number of sample(channel) elements per calData block.

member.name: T:Core.ItxtBxRxFloat
member.summary: allows TextBoxRxFloat, TextBoxUnitsAngle, TextBoxUnitsLength or TextBoxUnitsVelocity types to be used in a generic fashion.
member.remarks: enums cannot be used in a true generic fashion in C#. So this is my way of defining the enums UnitsAngle, UnitsLength, UnitsVelocity, etc. and using them in a generic fashion. The problem was that I couldn't use a generic enum to index into the ratio array in the 'float getUnitsRatio(UnitsVelocity unit)' method.

member.name: M:Core.Program.Main
member.summary: The main entry point for the application.

member.name: T:Core.TestPointRow
member.summary: a row in the Test Point Matrix, each Controller will have a least one Row. The array of float Values, the columns of the Test Point Matrix, is guarantee to always have at least one element. Values[] always contains the Values in the base unit. curAxis.listStrings. every value going into or out of Values is converted by the unitsRatio. This is to make it more effecient to The TestPointMatrix is a conceptual two dimensional array. Where the rows are the rows of the various controllers, and the columns are the elements of the rows. Thus, there is NOT a programatic object that represents the TestPointMatrix.

member.name: F:Core.TestPointRow.measuredValue

member.param: the index of the desired element in the row
member.returns: the value at the specified index, or the last value in this row

member.name: M:Core.TestPointRow.SetValueAtIndex(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: sets the value of the element at the index If the index is outside the possible elements for this row then the Values array is expanded. If the MaxValue is greater than the MinValue then strValue will be limited to the range between MinValue and MaxValue.
member.param: the index of the element to set to the value
the value

member.summary: return the enumBalBarMeasurmentIndices value based on the setting in cmboBxExcitation. Since the enum type used in any particular instantiation of this class (hence the Values associated with the cmboBxExcitation) may vary, I use the text value. This value is used by the hardware data collector to determine the offset into the data array returned by the HW.

member.name: P:Core.UchAi`3.ChannelBalBarMeasurementType
member.summary: get the current Bal Bar measurementType based on the contents of the measurementType comboBox. return the enumBalBarMeasurmentIndices value based on the setting in cmboBxMeasurement. Since the enum type used in any particular instantiation of this class (hence the Values associated with the cmboBxMeasurement) may vary, I use the text value. This value is used by the hardware data collector to determine the offset into the data array returned by the HW.

member.name: T:Core.UcmboBxEnum`1
member.summary:
a comboBox that uses Enum types
NOTE 1: in order to use this class with the UI designer, create an derived class that inherits this class and supplies the specific enum. Such as:
public partial class UcomboBoxMyEnum : UcomboBoxEnum<myEnum> {}
NOTE 2: Don't forget to call addCmboBxLabels(Tcfg value) in the constructor of the class that uses this. NOTE 3: the string "User_defined" in the Enum list will allow a user defined field to be entered.

member.name: M:Core.UcmboBxEnum`1.#ctor
member.summary:
a comboBox that uses Enum types
NOTE 1: in order to use this class with the UI designer, create an derived class that inherits this class and supplies the specific enum. Such as:
public partial class UcomboBoxMyEnum : UcomboBoxEnum<myEnum> {} NOTE 2: Don't forget to call addCmboBxLabels() or addCmboBxLabels(Tcfg value) in the constructor of the class that instantiates this class.

member.name: T:Core.ControllerGotoValues
member.summary: used by both the TestControlElement(s) in UTestControlProcess to position the controllers during a test. It contains the info necessary to position the controller for one TestPoint. and used by the manual controllers (UControllerSting) to position and configure the controllers used both directions, between UTool and FTool. When UTool sends it to FTool it tells the FTool controller where to set its vaues based on the ValuesArray in Values[one value per row of the controller] When FTool sends it to UTool it tells UTool the current ValuesArray of the Values, and if the controller setup is complete.

member.name: T:Core.ControllerSendTrigger
member.summary: used by the TestControlElement(s) in UTestControlProcess to cause the controllers to send a trigger pulse during a test. This happens after all of the controllers have signaled that they are at the next Test Point value, and before the data is collected. TODO - this is likely not quite right. > move controllers to next Test Point, > tell the data all data collectors that a trigger event is coming, and to collect data on that event > send the trigger event, this class, ControllerSendTrigger. > which will send the trigger causing the data collectors to collect and When TestControlElement sends it to FTool it tells the FTool controller where to set its vaues based on the ValuesArray in Values[one value per row of the controller] When FTool sends it to UTool it tells UTool the current ValuesArray of the Values, and if the controller setup is complete.
